title: React Native: Cross-Platform UI Framework
description: Build iOS and Android apps using React Native with JavaScript/TypeScript; leverage React patterns and npm ecosystem; fast iteration with hot reload.

cost.costNotes: Primary costs are development labor. App store fees unavoidable for mobile distribution.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: React Native apps run entirely on user devices with no mandatory infrastructure costs. Min=$0 represents development/testing with no paid services. Max=$100 covers optional services: Apple Developer Program ($99/year ≈ $8/month) + Google Play ($25 one-time ≈ $2/month amortized) + optional CI/CD services (GitHub Actions free tier, or CodeMagic/Bitrise at $50-90/month). Does not include backend APIs, analytics, or crash reporting services which are application-specific.
provenance.adoptionCost: Medium-high complexity due to: (1) React/JavaScript knowledge required - 20-40 hours learning if new to React ($1000-2000); (2) Native mobile concepts (navigation, gestures, permissions, platform-specific APIs) - 20-30 hours ($1000-1500); (3) Build toolchain setup (Xcode, Android Studio, or Expo) - 10-15 hours ($500-750); (4) Platform-specific debugging and testing across iOS/Android - 10-20 hours ($500-1000). Total: 60-105 hours at $50-100/dev hour blended rate. With AI coding assistants reducing implementation time by 30-40%, estimate $3500 for team with basic React knowledge, up to $6000 for team new to mobile development.
provenance.licenseCost: React Native is MIT licensed (fully open source, no restrictions). All core tooling is free: Metro bundler, React Native CLI, Expo (open source). Only distribution costs: Apple Developer Program ($99/year) and Google Play Console ($25 one-time) are operational costs, not licenses. No runtime royalties, usage fees, or enterprise licensing required.

provides.reasoning: React Native's core value proposition is enabling a single codebase to generate native UI for both iOS and Android platforms, eliminating the need for separate platform-specific codebases.
React Native provides a comprehensive library of mobile UI components that map to native platform widgets, offering access to platform-specific UI elements and mobile interaction patterns.
Fast Refresh/hot reload is a distinctive feature of React Native that enables rapid development iteration without full recompilation, significantly accelerating the development workflow.

React Native apps can leverage the vast npm package ecosystem for JavaScript/TypeScript libraries, providing access to thousands of packages for both general functionality and React Native-specific modules.

React Native provides a bridge mechanism that allows JavaScript code to integrate with native platform code, enabling access to platform-specific APIs and custom native modules when cross-platform abstractions are insufficient.

requires.reasoning: React Native is built on React principles including components, hooks, and state management, making React knowledge essential for effective development.
React Native applications are written in JavaScript or TypeScript, making proficiency in these languages fundamental to using the pattern.
React Native requires native toolchains including Xcode for iOS and Android Studio/Gradle for Android to compile, build, and package the final native applications.
Developers must understand iOS and Android deployment processes, including app store submission requirements, code signing, provisioning profiles, and platform-specific release configurations.

availability.reason: React Native is open source; documentation freely available.

supports_nfr.reason: Client-side framework availability limited by device issues, OS fragmentation, and crash handling.
React Native applications can be built to comply with GDPR through proper data handling.
React Native can support HIPAA-compliant applications with appropriate security controls.
React Native applications can implement CCPA compliance requirements through proper controls.
React Native can support SOX compliance through audit trails and data integrity measures.

supports_constraints.reason: React Native builds native mobile apps that run on device; cloud-agnostic client-side framework.
React Native exclusively uses JavaScript or TypeScript for application development.
React Native specifically targets mobile platforms (iOS and Android) for native app development.
React Native supports caching through libraries and AsyncStorage but does not require it.
React Native can integrate with async messaging systems but is not dependent on them.
React Native can incorporate AI inference through native modules or cloud APIs as needed.
React Native applications can support multi-tenancy through backend integration but do not require it.
React Native can trigger or display batch processing results but does not perform batch processing.
Client-side framework delegates distributed transaction coordination to backend services.
React Native supports real-time streaming through WebSockets and native modules but does not require it.
React Native can consume vector search APIs but does not provide native vector search capabilities.
React Native can integrate with document store backends but does not require them.
React Native provides AsyncStorage for local key-value storage and can integrate with external stores.
React Native can consume graph database APIs through backend integration but does not require them.
React Native can display time-series data from backend services but does not provide time-series storage.
Client-side UI framework is neutral to backend OLTP workload characteristics.
Client-side UI framework can visualize OLAP results but does not perform analytical processing.

navigation_library.description: Navigation solution: react-navigation (JS-based, most popular), react-native-navigation (native performance), or expo-router (file-based routing)

state_management.description: State management approach: react-hooks (built-in, simple), redux (predictable, verbose), mobx (reactive, less boilerplate), zustand (lightweight), recoil (atomic state)

native_modules.description: Native module strategy: expo-managed (easiest, limited native access), expo-bare (expo tools + custom native code), pure-react-native (full control, more setup)

styling_approach.description: Styling solution: stylesheet (built-in, performant), styled-components (CSS-in-JS), tailwind-rn (utility-first), native-base (component library)

platform_specific_code.description: Degree of platform-specific customization: minimal (maximize code sharing), moderate (platform-specific UI/UX), extensive (separate implementations per platform)
